summary.en: An appositive restates one concept in a different way. When a whole clause does that restating, it is an appositive clause — usually renaming an abstract noun like 'fact', 'question', or 'idea'.
summary.zh: 同位语就是用不同的方式把一个概念再说一遍。当一整个从句来做这件事，它就是同位语从句——通常是给抽象名词（如 fact、question、idea）换种说法再说一遍。

body.en: An appositive simply restates one concept in a different way, set off by a comma so the two don't blur together. In 'my teacher, Papa Rabbit', the teacher and Papa Rabbit are not two people — they are one concept, with 'Papa Rabbit' being another way of saying 'my teacher'. So 'Papa Rabbit' is the appositive of the subject 'teacher'.
body.zh: 同位语就是用不同的方式把同一个概念再说一遍，用逗号隔开，否则会乱在一起。在 my teacher, Papa Rabbit 中，老师和兔老爹不是两个人——它们是同一个概念，兔老爹只是“我的老师”的另一种说法。所以 Papa Rabbit 是主语 teacher 的同位语。

concepts.sourceRef: papa rabbit在这里是主语teacher的同位语

heading.zh: 从同位语到同位语从句

body.en: Extend the idea: when a whole clause acts as the appositive, that clause is an appositive clause. It typically modifies an abstract noun — precisely because the noun is abstract, you want a second way to spell it out. Because a clause marker shows where the clause begins, no comma is needed.
body.zh: 把这个概念引申到从句：当一整个从句来充当同位语时，这个从句就是同位语从句。它常修饰抽象的名词——正因为抽象，才要用另一种方式再说一遍。因为有引导词标明从句从哪里开始，所以不需要逗号隔开。

concepts.sourceRef: 当一个从句来充当同位语时，那么这个从句就是同位语从句

body.en: An appositive clause can carry known information (introduced by 'that': the fact that...) or unknown information (introduced by 'whether', 'who', etc.: the question whether..., the idea who...). Either way, the clause renames the noun it follows.
body.zh: 同位语从句既可以包含已知信息（由 that 引导：the fact that...），也可以包含未知信息（由 whether、who 等引导：the question whether...、the idea who...）。无论哪种，从句都是把它前面的名词重新说一遍。

concepts.sourceRef: 同位语从句当然也可以包含未知信息

body.en: The Chinese translation usually reorders the words, because Chinese has no clause markers. 'The fact that the rabbit ate the carrot' becomes '兔子吃了胡萝卜这个事实' — the clause comes before the noun, not after it.
body.zh: 中文翻译的词序往往和英文不一样，因为中文没有引导词的概念。the fact that the rabbit ate the carrot 译成“兔子吃了胡萝卜这个事实”——从句放在名词前面，而不是后面。

concepts.sourceRef: 中文翻译的词序往往是和英文不一样的

examples.text: My teacher, Papa Rabbit, likes carrots.

note.en: 'Papa Rabbit' is just another way of saying 'my teacher' — the appositive of the subject, set off by commas.
note.zh: “Papa Rabbit”只是“my teacher”的另一种说法——主语的同位语，用逗号隔开。

examples.text: The fact that the rabbit ate the carrot did not surprise me.

note.en: 'The fact' and 'that the rabbit ate the carrot' are one concept said two ways. The marker 'that' shows where the clause starts, so no comma is needed.
note.zh: “the fact”和“that the rabbit ate the carrot”是同一个概念的两种说法。引导词 that 标明从句起点，所以不需要逗号。

examples.text: The question whether the rabbit would eat the carrot is on all our minds.

annotations.quote: the rabbit would eat the carrot

note.en: Unknown information: 'whether the rabbit would eat the carrot' restates the subject 'question'.
note.zh: 未知信息：“whether the rabbit would eat the carrot”把主语“question”又说了一遍。

examples.text: I have no idea who ate the carrot.

note.en: 'Idea' is the object; 'who ate the carrot' redefines it — an appositive clause of the object.
note.zh: “idea”是宾语；“who ate the carrot”重新定义它——宾语的同位语从句。

examples.text: I am sure of the fact that there's a carrot on the table.

annotations.quote: there's a carrot on the table

note.en: Rewriting 'I am sure that...' as 'I am sure of the fact that...' makes it a pure appositive clause renaming the object 'fact'.
note.zh: 把“I am sure that...”改成“I am sure of the fact that...”，就成了纯粹的同位语从句，给宾语“fact”换种说法。

prompt.en: In 'The fact that the rabbit ate the carrot did not surprise me', what does 'that the rabbit ate the carrot' do?
prompt.zh: 在“The fact that the rabbit ate the carrot did not surprise me”中，“that the rabbit ate the carrot”起什么作用？

text.en: It restates the noun 'fact' — an appositive clause
text.zh: 把名词“fact”再说一遍——同位语从句

text.en: It is the subject of the sentence

explain.en: The clause and 'the fact' are one concept said two ways, so the clause is the appositive of 'fact'.
explain.zh: 从句和“the fact”是同一概念的两种说法，所以从句是“fact”的同位语。

prompt.en: Why does an appositive clause not need a comma, while a noun appositive like 'Papa Rabbit' does?
prompt.zh: 为什么同位语从句不需要逗号，而像“Papa Rabbit”这样的名词同位语却需要？

text.en: Clauses are never separated by punctuation
A clause marker already shows where the clause begins
text.zh: 引导词已经标明了从句的起点

text.en: The clause is too long for a comma
text.zh: 从句太长，不适合用逗号

explain.en: Because a marker like 'that' or 'whether' marks the start of the clause, no comma is needed to separate it.
explain.zh: 因为 that、whether 等引导词标明了从句的起点，所以不需要逗号来隔开。

prompt.en: Complete the appositive clause: 'The question ____ the rabbit would eat the carrot is on all our minds.'
prompt.zh: 补全同位语从句：“The question ____ the rabbit would eat the carrot is on all our minds.”

explain.en: Unknown yes/no information after 'question' is introduced by 'whether'.
explain.zh: “question”后面表示是否的未知信息，由“whether”引导。

quizzes.sentence: I have no idea, who ate the carrot.
quizzes.fix: I have no idea who ate the carrot.

explain.en: An appositive clause takes no comma — the marker 'who' already shows where the clause begins.
explain.zh: 同位语从句不加逗号——引导词“who”已经标明从句的起点。

lines.speaker: Wolf
lines.text: My teacher, Papa Rabbit, eats too many carrots.

note.en: A noun appositive, set off by commas.
note.zh: 名词同位语，用逗号隔开。

lines.speaker: Rabbit
lines.text: The fact that I eat carrots does not surprise anyone.

note.en: A 'that' appositive clause renaming 'fact' — no comma.
note.zh: 由 that 引导、重述“fact”的同位语从句——不加逗号。

lines.speaker: Wolf
lines.text: But the question whether you share them stays on my mind.

note.zh: 由 whether 引导的未知信息。

lines.speaker: Rabbit
lines.text: I have no idea who told you that.

note.en: 'Who told you that' redefines the object 'idea'.
note.zh: “who told you that”重新定义宾语“idea”。
